parts.text:
Here's a summary of the town hall meeting:

The meeting was a kick-off for a new project focused on building useful and valuable multi-agent experiences, drawing lessons from last year's successful "Agent Bar" demo at Google Cloud Next. The project aims to evolve the "build your own agent" concept into something more robust and practical for various industries and use cases, moving beyond "toy" applications.

**Key Speakers and Roles:**

*   **Rob:** UX expert, responsible for designing the user experience, including the visual canvas for multi-agent assembly.
*   **Nivea:** Involved in project management, task organization, and meeting scheduling.
*   **Jackie and Camelia:** Contributing to the overall experience, setup, and operational aspects of the project, including non-technical assets for events like "Next."
*   **Quinn:** Involved with the agent catalog and potentially the gamification aspect of the project.
*   **Brad:** Expressed interest in using the developed tool for customer demos beyond just top-of-funnel events.

**Project Goals and Approach:**

The core idea is to create a system that allows users to either follow a "guided path" for specific industry solutions (e.g., fraud detection in finance) or a "choose your own adventure" path where AI recommends agents based on natural language problem descriptions. This involves using composable and reusable components from the GCC agent catalog and ADK samples. The plan is to use a root agent to orchestrate multiple sub-agents, with Gemini 3.0 and Agent Engine playing key roles.

**Project Timeline and Management:**

The team has a tight timeline of approximately nine weeks to have a working product ready for the "Next" conference in mid-March. The project is structured with initial phases for kickoff and tech setup (two weeks), followed by implementation (six to seven weeks), and then launch readiness. Project management will leverage Asana for tasks and... [truncated]

Mocking up what it could look like. I think we'll have similar type of work. We could take, you know, motivation from what we did last year. We don't have to start from scratch. We know what worked um and we've, you know, got a similar type of experience with, you know, with some new bells and whistles. Um, we'll bring in some app dev folks as well. Those are being staffed now. GST is pretty I guess at capacity. So we'll get those either next week or in a couple days and they will be primarily like angular experts. So no worries Mike. We also recorded this as well Mike so you didn't really miss much. So the the whole idea here is we'll have a team of front end engineers. Rob will be mocking up what this could look like and he'll be working with us. And then those angular experts will work with us. And again the but base we currently already have uh to build this out um from a front end experience. And then from the back end, we've got a a whole host of team members that have experience with with that and AI and agents. Um, you know, you already heard from a couple of them. From a TL perspective, that's the role I'll be playing. Um, I know a lot of us are staff probably at 100%... [truncated]
